prompt: I have a simple generative model: data is drawn from a Gaussian with unknown mean mu and standard deviation sigma. I can simulate from this model easily. I want to use BayesFlow to train an amortized posterior estimator so I can do instant inference on new datasets of 50 i.i.d. observations. Set this up end to end — from simulator to diagnostics to inference on a test dataset.
assertions: Defines a prior function returning mu and sigma as a dict
Defines an observation model function that generates N i.i.d. draws given mu and sigma
Uses bf.make_simulator() to create the simulator (not a manual Python loop)
Uses bf.Adapter() with an explicit adapter chain (not just naming kwargs)
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) in the adapter for the positive parameter
Calls .as_set() on the observations in the adapter
Uses a SetTransformer as the summary network (not DeepSet, not None)
Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
Uses FlowMatching, DiffusionModel, or StableConsistencyModel as the inference network
Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Saves history.history as JSON and runs inspect_training or equivalent convergence check
Uses workflow.simulate() for generating test data (not a Python loop)
Runs check_diagnostics or manually checks ECE and NRMSE against house thresholds
Accesses posterior samples by original parameter names (mu, sigma), NOT 'inference_variables'
